_provenance.source: AerialWaste MCML m4 split (6 classes), AW v2.0 dataset
_provenance.note: Cues hand-written for aerial-view identification. m4 is a finer-grained alternative to m2: rubble bundles in 'excavated earth'; 'Scrap' splits out from m2's 'Bulky/Containers'; 'Sludge-Zootechnical' is the wet-biological class largely absent from DW paper-10; 'Wood' and 'Tires' map cleanly to DW Pallets/Tyres but include a wider object set; 'Other waste' is the catch-all.

Rubble/excavated earth and rocks.ewc_description: Construction & demolition rubble plus excavated earth/rocks.
Rubble/excavated earth and rocks.aerial_cue: piles of broken concrete, bricks or stones AND brown/grey soil/excavated earth heaps — irregular, dusty; sometimes mounds of soil mixed with rubble

Scrap.ewc_description: Ferrous, non-ferrous and mixed-alloy scrap metal.
Scrap.aerial_cue: tangled metal fragments — twisted, rusty-orange or shiny — in heterogeneous piles; no regular geometry, mix of small cm-scale pieces and larger metal objects

Sludge-Zootechnical waste-Manure.ewc_description: Wet biological / agricultural waste: sludge, manure, zootechnical residues.
Sludge-Zootechnical waste-Manure.aerial_cue: wet brown or dark-black sludge / manure pools — smooth, sometimes liquid surface, often near barns or open ponds; can look almost mirror-like when freshly poured

Wood.ewc_description: Wooden waste: pallets, planks, branches, demolished timber.
Wood.aerial_cue: wooden boards, pallets, lumber stacks, tree branches — brown / light-yellow wood texture, often rectangular planks or slatted pallet patterns, sometimes piled chaotically

Tires.aerial_cue: dark black circular rubber discs ~60 cm wide — donut/ring shape from above, single, in dense stacks, or half-buried

Other waste.ewc_description: Heterogeneous mixed waste not falling into the other 5 categories.
Other waste.aerial_cue: heterogeneous heap mixing textile, plastic, paper, packaging, food waste — multicoloured, mound-shaped, no single dominant material type
